message:
fix: 7 directed-only fns raise correct exception class on undirected (br-r37-c1-n7rgh)

Audit of all 53 nx functions decorated with
``@not_implemented_for('undirected')`` found 7 functions that
raised the wrong exception class on undirected input:

  directed_edge_swap          fnx: NetworkXError
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  in_degree_centrality(MG)    fnx: AttributeError
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  out_degree_centrality(MG)   fnx: AttributeError
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  moral_graph                 fnx: AttributeError
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  reverse_view                fnx: NetworkXError
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  triad_type                  fnx: NetworkXError
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  triadic_census              fnx: NetworkXError
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented

NetworkXNotImplemented is NOT a subclass of NetworkXError —
both descend from NetworkXException independently — so drop-in
code that does ``except NetworkXNotImplemented`` (or
``pytest.raises(NetworkXNotImplemented)``) would not trigger on
fnx. The AttributeError cases are even worse: those happened
because the fnx wrapper fell through to a directed-only graph
method (``G.in_degree``, ``G.predecessors``) without first
checking the type.

Fix
---
``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py``: add
``if not G.is_directed(): raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not
implemented for undirected type")`` at the top of each wrapper.
Also normalized ``directed_edge_swap``'s post-type-guard size
checks (``len(G) < 4`` → NetworkXError "DiGraph has fewer than
four nodes."; ``number_of_edges < 3`` → "DiGraph has fewer than
3 edges") to match nx's preconditions.

Also updated ``tests/python/test_triadic_census_order_parity.py``:
the existing ``test_undirected_input_raises_networkxerror``
asserted ``NetworkXError`` which no longer matches; replaced
with ``NetworkXNotImplemented`` and added the nx side-by-side
check.

Tests
-----
New ``tests/python/test_undirected_guard_exception_type_parity.py``:
- test_undirected_input_raises_not_implemented (parametrized
  over 7 functions × {Graph, MultiGraph} = 14 cases)
- test_undirected_caught_by_nx_class (parametrized over 7
  functions; drop-in catchability)
- test_directed_edge_swap_directed_input_size_check (the
  post-guard NetworkXError on <4 nodes still works)
- 5 regression guards on directed inputs (one per affected
  function whose happy path needed verification)

Full sweep
----------
6823 passed, 0 failed (was 6796 before — +27 new regression cases).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -106,6 +106,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-mnrem","title":"parity: fnx.approximation.* exposed (G, *args, **kwargs) catch-all signatures, hiding all real kwargs","description":"_ApproximationNamespace dynamically wrapped each approximation function as (G, *args, **kwargs), so inspect.signature(fnx.approximation.X) returned a useless catch-all for every function. nx exposes the documented per-function signatures (average_clustering(G, trials, seed), christofides(G, weight, tree), greedy_tsp(G, weight, source), etc). 27 functions affected. Drop-in callers couldn't see what kwargs are accepted via introspection, IDEs and type-checkers couldn't help, and typos passed silently into nx's **kwargs handling. Fix: functools.wraps(nx_func) on the wrapper so the original signature/name/docstring are preserved. 30 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:41:51.191871760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:42:37.315185389Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:42:37.313339806Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 63a0163. _ApproximationNamespace.__getattr__ now uses functools.wraps so every fnx.approximation.X carries nx's exact signature, name, and docstring. 30 parity tests freeze the contract; all 27 previously-mismatched functions now pass introspection.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-mpv5x","title":"parity: __str__ drift on view classes and Graph (name dropped, repr-style instead of bare-data)","description":"Several __str__ implementations diverge from nx: NodeView/EdgeView/DegreeView/NodeDataView/EdgeDataView/AtlasView/AdjacencyView fall back to __repr__ (e.g. 'NodeView((0, 1, 2))') instead of nx's bare-data form ('[0, 1, 2]'). Graph.__str__ ignores the graph name attribute: fnx returns 'Graph with 0 nodes and 0 edges' even when G.graph['name'] = 'G' is set; nx returns 'Graph named \'G\' with 0 nodes and 0 edges'. Drop-in code that does print(G), print(G.nodes), or asserts on str(view) gets different output. Fix: add explicit __str__ methods that return the bare-data forms matching nx exactly; fix Graph.__str__ to include the name when set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:36:59.610629652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:43:54.211977409Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T08:43:54.209976079Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 73c840f. View __str__ returns bare data (list/dict) matching nx; Graph.__str__ includes name when set. 15 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-msf5j","title":"DiGraph/Multi*.edges() returns frozen list, not live view (vs nx OutEdgeView)","description":"fnx.DiGraph.edges(), fnx.MultiGraph.edges(), and fnx.MultiDiGraph.edges() return a frozen list snapshot at call time. nx returns a live view (OutEdgeView / MultiEdgeDataView / OutMultiEdgeDataView) that reflects subsequent graph mutations. Drop-in code that captures a view and then mutates the graph silently breaks under fnx.

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.DiGraph([(1,2),(2,3)])
  >>> v = g.edges()
  >>> g.add_edge(3, 4)
  >>> list(v)
  [(1, 2), (2, 3)]            # nx returns [(1,2),(2,3),(3,4)]

Root cause: _DiGraphEdgeView.__call__ at python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:808 always builds and returns a list. Same pattern in _MultiGraphEdgeView.__call__ (returns _EdgeListWithSetAlgebra, a list subclass). nx's EdgeView.__call__(nbunch=None, data=False, default=None) returns self when called with default args.

Fix: short-circuit __call__ to return self when nbunch=None and data=False; keep current list-building behavior for the data=True/'attr'/nbunch-filtered paths (those are correctly modeled as snapshot views in nx via EdgeDataView, but matching that in full is out of scope here).

Note: a parallel defect exists for MultiGraph.nodes() / MultiDiGraph.nodes() (Rust-side NodeView class) — separate bead.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T04:33:07.797286806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T04:40:27.272422580Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T04:40:27.269651843Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-n7rgh","title":"7 directed-only fns raise wrong exception type on undirected input","description":"Audit of all 53 nx functions decorated with @not_implemented_for('undirected') found 7 functions that raise the wrong exception type on undirected input (Graph and/or MultiGraph):

  directed_edge_swap          fnx: NetworkXError ('requires a directed graph')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  in_degree_centrality(MG)    fnx: AttributeError ('no attr in_degree')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  out_degree_centrality(MG)   fnx: AttributeError ('no attr out_degree')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  moral_graph                 fnx: AttributeError ('no attr predecessors')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  reverse_view                fnx: NetworkXError ('Cannot reverse an undirected graph.')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  triad_type                  fnx: NetworkXError ('triad_type requires DiGraph')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented
  triadic_census              fnx: NetworkXError ('requires a directed graph')
                               nx: NetworkXNotImplemented

Drop-in code that does pytest.raises(NetworkXNotImplemented) doesn't trigger on fnx — neither NetworkXError nor AttributeError is a subclass of NetworkXNotImplemented.

Fix: add if not G.is_directed(): raise NetworkXNotImplemented('not implemented for undirected type') at the top of each wrapper, matching nx's @not_implemented_for('undirected') decorator.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T08:58:14.726580507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T08:58:50.100636720Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-nhz31","title":"test_coverage_gaps: refactor recent direct-nx wrappers + regenerate coverage.md","description":"Two pre-existing failures in test_coverage_gaps.py:
- test_public_coverage_has_no_networkx_delegated_exports: 54 exports classify as NX_DELEGATED. The test asserts == 0.
- test_generated_coverage_matrix_document_is_current: docs/coverage.md is out of date.

Most of the 54 NX_DELEGATED items pre-date my recent work. But my recent fixes added 6 more by directly importing nx in public functions:
- intersection (br-r37-c1-saf8a)
- k_truss (br-r37-c1-3qopf)
- maximum_spanning_tree (br-r37-c1-rtak4)
- minimum_spanning_tree (br-r37-c1-rtak4)
- transitive_closure_dag (br-r37-c1-utmy6)
- transitive_reduction (br-r37-c1-utmy6)

The project convention (used by stochastic_block_model, hoffman_singleton_graph, etc.) is to keep the nx import inside a private _xxx_impl helper so the public function is classified as PY_WRAPPER instead of NX_DELEGATED.

Scope of this bead: refactor the 6 I recently introduced to follow the convention, and regenerate docs/coverage.md. The other 48 pre-existing items are out of scope (separate refactor).

After this bead, the count drops from 54 → 48, the second test passes if docs are regenerated, and the first test still fails but no worse than before.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:53:43.443005498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:58:10.770550620Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:58:10.768479207Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ntkdn","title":"parity: random_sequence powerlaw/zipf/cumulative_distribution/is_valid_tree_degree_sequence (4 fns)","description":"Four random-sequence utilities from networkx.utils.random_sequence not surfaced: powerlaw_sequence(n, exponent=2.0, seed=None), zipf_rv(alpha, xmin=1, seed=None), cumulative_distribution(distribution), is_valid_tree_degree_sequence(degree_sequence). Used by stochastic generators and degree-seq sanity checks. Add wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level + __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:04:43.303897526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:37.064336059Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:37.062433818Z","close_reason":"Shipped in b08418c: powerlaw_sequence/zipf_rv/cumulative_distribution/is_valid_tree_degree_sequence wrappers; all 4 verified against nx (n=20 exp=2.5 seed=42 powerlaw match exactly, cumulative_distribution = [0,0.5,0.8,1.0]).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-nzo8r","title":"random_regular_graph FailClosed for ~20% of seeds where nx succeeds","description":"fnx.random_regular_graph(d=4, n=10) raises ValueError(FailClosed{...}) for seeds 2,3,7,9,13,33,34,42,43,44 (10/50 = 20%) where nx succeeds. Root cause: crates/fnx-generators/src/lib.rs:670 uses a naive stub-pairing algorithm that throws away the entire attempt on any duplicate/self-edge and gives up after 100 attempts. nx's algorithm (see __init__.py:32986-33024) tracks unmatched stubs in potential_edges and only restarts on _suitable failure, which makes it converge in nearly all cases. Port nx's smarter inner loop into the Rust impl.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:23:16.888694411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:37:06.376266393Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:37:06.373143578Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -10356,6 +10356,13 @@ def in_degree_centrality(G, *, backend=None, **backend_kwargs):
     already counts multi-edges correctly) for multigraphs to match nx.
     """
     _validate_backend_dispatch_keywords("in_degree_centrality", backend, backend_kwargs)
+    # br-r37-c1-n7rgh: nx is @not_implemented_for('undirected'). The
+    # MultiGraph (undirected + multi) branch fell through to
+    # G.in_degree() which doesn't exist on undirected and raised
+    # AttributeError. Add the type guard so MG and Graph alike raise
+    # NetworkXNotImplemented matching nx.
+    if not G.is_directed():
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for undirected type")
     if G.is_multigraph():
         n = len(G)
         if n <= 1:
@@ -10378,6 +10385,9 @@ def out_degree_centrality(G, *, backend=None, **backend_kwargs):
     Rust path undercounted parallel edges on MultiDiGraph.
     """
     _validate_backend_dispatch_keywords("out_degree_centrality", backend, backend_kwargs)
+    # br-r37-c1-n7rgh: same undirected guard as in_degree_centrality.
+    if not G.is_directed():
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for undirected type")
     if G.is_multigraph():
         n = len(G)
         if n <= 1:
@@ -19010,8 +19020,12 @@ def triadic_census(G, nodelist=None):
     dict
         ``{triad_type: count}`` for all 16 types.
     """
+    # br-r37-c1-n7rgh: nx is @not_implemented_for('undirected') and
+    # raises NetworkXNotImplemented; previously fnx raised
+    # NetworkXError which is the parent class but not catchable as
+    # NetworkXNotImplemented.
     if not G.is_directed():
-        raise NetworkXError("triadic_census requires a directed graph")
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for undirected type")
     if nodelist is None:
         raw = _fnx.triadic_census_rust(G)
         # br-r37-c1-v9m7b: nx returns the 16 triad types in canonical
@@ -19059,7 +19073,14 @@ def triad_type(G):
     -------
     str
         One of the 16 MAN triad type codes.
+
+    br-r37-c1-n7rgh: nx is @not_implemented_for('undirected'); add
+    the type guard before the 3-node check so undirected inputs
+    raise NetworkXNotImplemented (matching nx) instead of the
+    Rust binding's NetworkXError.
     """
+    if not G.is_directed():
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for undirected type")
     nodes = list(G.nodes())
     if len(nodes) != 3:
         raise NetworkXError("triad_type requires exactly 3 nodes")
@@ -19175,8 +19196,16 @@ def directed_edge_swap(G, *, nswap=1, max_tries=100, seed=None):
 
     rng = _random.Random(seed)
 
+    # br-r37-c1-n7rgh: nx is @not_implemented_for('undirected') and
+    # raises NetworkXNotImplemented (catchable via that subclass);
+    # previously fnx raised the parent NetworkXError class.
     if not G.is_directed():
-        raise NetworkXError("directed_edge_swap requires a directed graph")
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for undirected type")
+    # nx contract: at least 4 nodes and 3 edges, else NetworkXError.
+    if len(G) < 4:
+        raise NetworkXError("DiGraph has fewer than four nodes.")
+    if G.number_of_edges() < 3:
+        raise NetworkXError("DiGraph has fewer than 3 edges")
     if G.number_of_edges() < 2:
         return G
 
@@ -21821,9 +21850,16 @@ def moral_graph(G):
 
     For each node, connect all pairs of its predecessors (marry co-parents),
     then return the undirected version.
+
+    br-r37-c1-n7rgh: nx is @not_implemented_for('undirected'); pre-fix
+    fnx fell through to G.predecessors() which raises AttributeError
+    on undirected. Add the explicit type guard.
     """
     from itertools import combinations
 
+    if not G.is_directed():
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for undirected type")
+
     H = Graph()
     for node in G.nodes():
         H.add_node(node, **dict(G.nodes[node]))
@@ -28936,7 +28972,15 @@ def filter_edge(u, v):
 
 
 def reverse_view(G):
-    """View with reversed edges."""
+    """View with reversed edges.
+
+    br-r37-c1-n7rgh: nx is @not_implemented_for('undirected'); the
+    underlying reverse() raises NetworkXError for undirected
+    inputs, but nx raises NetworkXNotImplemented before reaching
+    that code.
+    """
+    if not G.is_directed():
+        raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for undirected type")
     return reverse(G, copy=False)

@@ -78,8 +78,21 @@ def test_triadic_census_with_nodelist_kwarg():
 
 
 @needs_nx
-def test_undirected_input_raises_networkxerror():
-    """Regression: the directed-only guard must not regress."""
+def test_undirected_input_raises_not_implemented():
+    """Regression: the directed-only guard must not regress.
+
+    br-r37-c1-n7rgh: triadic_census now raises
+    NetworkXNotImplemented (matching nx's
+    @not_implemented_for('undirected') decorator) instead of the
+    parent NetworkXError. NetworkXNotImplemented is a subclass of
+    NetworkXException, NOT of NetworkXError, so the previous
+    test would have to be updated when the guard's exception
+    class was tightened.
+    """
     G = fnx.path_graph(3)
-    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError):
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXNotImplemented):
         fnx.triadic_census(G)
+    # Also verify nx's behavior matches.
+    import networkx as nx
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXNotImplemented):
+        nx.triadic_census(nx.path_graph(3))

@@ -0,0 +1,136 @@
+"""Parity for @not_implemented_for('undirected') guards.
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-n7rgh. Audit of all 53 nx functions decorated with
+``@not_implemented_for('undirected')`` found 7 functions that
+raised the WRONG exception type on undirected input — usually
+``NetworkXError`` (parent class) or ``AttributeError`` (when the
+fnx wrapper fell through to a directed-only graph method).
+
+Drop-in code that does ``pytest.raises(NetworkXNotImplemented)``
+wouldn't trigger because NetworkXNotImplemented is NOT a subclass
+of NetworkXError; both descend from NetworkXException
+independently.
+
+Affected functions (all now raise NetworkXNotImplemented):
+- directed_edge_swap
+- in_degree_centrality (MultiGraph specifically)
+- out_degree_centrality (MultiGraph specifically)
+- moral_graph
+- reverse_view
+- triad_type
+- triadic_census
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+
+UNDIR_GUARDED_FNS = [
+    "directed_edge_swap",
+    "in_degree_centrality",
+    "out_degree_centrality",
+    "moral_graph",
+    "reverse_view",
+    "triad_type",
+    "triadic_census",
+]
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("fn_name", UNDIR_GUARDED_FNS)
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("cls_name", ["Graph", "MultiGraph"])
+def test_undirected_input_raises_not_implemented(fn_name, cls_name):
+    G = getattr(fnx, cls_name)([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = getattr(nx, cls_name)([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    f_fn = getattr(fnx, fn_name)
+    n_fn = getattr(nx, fn_name)
+    with pytest.raises(
+        fnx.NetworkXNotImplemented,
+        match=r"^not implemented for undirected type$",
+    ):
+        f_fn(G)
+    with pytest.raises(
+        nx.NetworkXNotImplemented,
+        match=r"^not implemented for undirected type$",
+    ):
+        n_fn(GX)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("fn_name", UNDIR_GUARDED_FNS)
+def test_undirected_caught_by_nx_class(fn_name):
+    """Drop-in: each fnx-raised NetworkXNotImplemented must be
+    catchable via ``except nx.NetworkXNotImplemented``."""
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    try:
+        getattr(fnx, fn_name)(G)
+    except nx.NetworkXNotImplemented:
+        return
+    pytest.fail(
+        f"fnx.{fn_name} should raise NetworkXNotImplemented on undirected input"
+    )
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_directed_edge_swap_directed_input_size_check():
+    """Pre-existing nx contract: <4 nodes raises NetworkXError
+    (NOT NetworkXNotImplemented). Verify both libraries agree on
+    the post-type-guard size check."""
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(1, 2)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match=r"fewer than four nodes"):
+        fnx.directed_edge_swap(G)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match=r"fewer than four nodes"):
+        nx.directed_edge_swap(GX)
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Regression — directed inputs continue to work
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_in_degree_centrality_directed_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 1)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 1)])
+    assert fnx.in_degree_centrality(G) == nx.in_degree_centrality(GX)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_out_degree_centrality_directed_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 1)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 1)])
+    assert fnx.out_degree_centrality(G) == nx.out_degree_centrality(GX)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_moral_graph_directed_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(0, 2), (1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(0, 2), (1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    f = sorted(fnx.moral_graph(G).edges())
+    n = sorted(nx.moral_graph(GX).edges())
+    assert f == n
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_reverse_view_directed_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    assert sorted(fnx.reverse_view(G).edges()) == sorted(nx.reverse_view(GX).edges())
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_triadic_census_directed_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 1)])
+    GX = nx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 1)])
+    assert fnx.triadic_census(G) == nx.triadic_census(GX)
